title: tanacetum silaifolium
intro: Tanacetum silaifolium, also known as Tansy, is a perennial herb native to Eurasia and North Africa, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tansy include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Tansy is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
